title: andy-cli headless event stream (v1)
description: NDJSON envelope emitted by `andy-cli run --headless --config <path>`. One JSON object per line on stdout (or the FIFO named in event_sink.path). Consumed by andy-containers' AP6 fan-out to `andy.containers.events.run.{run_id}.progress`. Schema is additive: consumers MUST tolerate unknown `kind` values and unknown `data` fields.

schema_version.description: Schema version pin. v2 will ship a new file.

ts.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 timestamp with offset, e.g. 2026-04-25T22:31:14.123+00:00. Always wall-clock UTC from the agent process.

data.description: Per-kind payload. See $defs for the closed shape per kind. Producers MAY emit additional fields; consumers MUST tolerate them.

call_id.description: Producer-assigned id, unique within this run; pairs with the matching tool_call_finished.

args_digest.description: SHA-256 hex of the canonical-JSON-serialized arg object. Args themselves are not emitted to keep the event stream cheap and to avoid leaking secrets.

tool_call_finished.description: Emitted once per tool_call_started, ONLY after the tool's actual execution has completed (succeeded, failed, was denied by the permission engine, timed out, or was cancelled). `duration_ms` is measured start-to-finish by the producer. Exactly one tool_call_finished pairs with each tool_call_started via `call_id`.

ok.description: true only when outcome=success. Any non-success terminal state (failed, denied, timed_out, cancelled) is ok=false. A denied or still-running tool is never reported ok=true.

outcome.description: The terminal state of the actual execution. `denied` is a permission-engine refusal (distinct from `failed`, an execution error); `timed_out` is a per-tool timeout / resource-limit abort; `cancelled` is a caller/run cancellation. Additive field; consumers MUST tolerate its absence and unknown values.

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock milliseconds measured by the producer from tool_call_started to the observed completion. Never fabricated as 0.

result_digest.description: SHA-256 hex of the canonical-JSON-serialized result. Optional; producers MAY omit on error.

error.description: Short error string when ok=false. Producers SHOULD redact or truncate; consumers MUST NOT key on its content.

tool_usage_audit.description: AX.4 (rivoli-ai/conductor#2091) end-of-run tool-usage audit. Records the distinct tools the agent actually invoked during the run and, for each, whether the permission engine permitted it under the injected allow-list. Lets an external verifier (AX.10) confirm that only permitted tools ran. Emitted once, just before `finished`.

allowed_tools.description: The per-run permission allow-list that was injected (config.permissions.allowed_tools). Empty when none was supplied.

tools.description: One entry per distinct tool invoked during the run.

invocations.description: How many times the agent invoked this tool.

permitted.description: true ⇒ the permission engine permitted this tool (in the allow-list or an auto-allowed read-only built-in); false ⇒ the tool was denied (fail-closed) at execution time.

required_action_verification.description: Machine-checkable verification of config.required_actions against actual terminal tool outcomes. Emitted once when requirements were declared, before output publication on a converged run and before finished on every other run.

satisfied.description: true only when every declared requirement has at least its configured number of successful matching calls.

command_digest.description: SHA-256 digest of command_equals when present. The raw command is not emitted.

calls.description: Bounded terminal evidence for matching calls. Each id pairs with the normal tool_call_started/tool_call_finished events.

path.description: Absolute path to the file written by AQ4's atomic write.

fatal.description: true ⇒ terminal error; the next event will be `finished` and the process will exit non-zero.

exit_code.description: Mirrors the process exit code (HeadlessExitCode in src/Andy.Cli/HeadlessConfig).

iterations.description: Number of LLM/tool turns executed before terminating.
